topic: Areas of Inquiry: Music for Dramatic Impact, Movement and Entertainment
subject: music

items.q: What does the time signature 6/8 indicate?
items.correct: Two beats per bar, each beat divided into three (compound duple).
items.misconception: Six beats per bar, all equal in stress.
items.why: Students count 6/8 as six equal beats. In practice it's felt as TWO dotted-crotchet beats subdivided into three quavers each - compound duple, not simple sextuple.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.q: Which orchestral family does the saxophone belong to?
items.misconception: Brass (because it is made of brass).
items.why: Students classify by material. Instrument families are defined by SOUND PRODUCTION: woodwinds use a reed or air across an edge. The sax uses a reed, so despite the brass body, it's woodwind.

items.q: In what year was the world music label adopted by indie labels?
items.why: Correct answer: 1987. “1972” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many areas of inquiry frame IBDP Music since 2020?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 areas. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
